channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Elevate Government Affairs Internship 2026: Paid Full-Time Government Affairs Internship in Federal Policy, Congress, Transportation, Technology, and Energy Policy
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Are you passionate about federal policy, government relations, legislative affairs, transportation policy, technology regulation, telecommunications, or energy policy? Elevate Government Affairs is offering an exciting paid internship opportunity for aspiring government affairs professionals seeking hands-on experience in one of Washington’s most dynamic policy environments. This full-time, paid, and in-person internship provides participants with the opportunity […]

The post Elevate Government Affairs Internship 2026: Paid Full-Time Government Affairs Internship in Federal Policy, Congress, Transportation, Technology, and Energy Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about federal policy, government relations, legislative affairs, transportation policy, technology regulation, telecommunications, or energy policy? Elevate Government Affairs is offering an exciting paid internship opportunity for aspiring government affairs professionals seeking hands-on experience in one of Washington’s most dynamic policy environments.

This full-time, paid, and in-person internship provides participants with the opportunity to work directly alongside experienced bipartisan government affairs professionals while gaining valuable exposure to congressional processes, policy analysis, client engagement, legislative tracking, and strategic advocacy.

The internship is ideal for recent graduates, bachelor’s degree holders, and emerging professionals who want to build a career in government affairs, public policy, lobbying, legislative affairs, political consulting, or public administration.

Successful candidates must be available to begin work on August 31, 2026, and remain in the role through December 18, 2026.

About Elevate Government Affairs

Elevate Government Affairs was established by three highly experienced government relations professionals with a vision of helping organizations successfully navigate an increasingly complex government affairs landscape.

The firm was created to address a growing challenge faced by organizations across sectors: ensuring their voices are heard in a policy environment that is becoming more competitive and complex.

Elevate’s mission centers on:

  • Delivering measurable policy outcomes for clients
  • Building customized government affairs strategies
  • Providing personalized client engagement
  • Creating innovative advocacy solutions
  • Supporting clients at every level of government
  • Leveraging bipartisan expertise to achieve policy goals

What distinguishes Elevate Government Affairs is the depth of experience within its leadership and professional team. Collectively, the firm’s professionals possess more than 125 years of combined experience working with:

  • Members of the U.S. House of Representatives
  • Members of the U.S. Senate
  • Congressional Committees
  • Executive Branch agencies
  • Public policy organizations
  • Government relations initiatives

The organization emphasizes bipartisan collaboration and strategic innovation, enabling clients to effectively engage policymakers and decision-makers.

Internship Overview

This internship offers a unique opportunity to gain practical experience in federal policy and government affairs while working directly with professionals who shape public policy strategies.

Interns will become integral members of a collaborative team and contribute to projects involving:

  • Transportation policy
  • Infrastructure policy
  • Technology policy
  • Telecommunications policy
  • Energy policy
  • Congressional affairs
  • Legislative analysis
  • Client engagement

The internship combines research, policy monitoring, communication, strategic analysis, and client support responsibilities.

What Interns Will Do

Participants will be actively involved in the day-to-day operations of the firm.

1. Policy Research and Analysis

Interns will conduct detailed policy research to support client projects and internal strategic initiatives.

Research responsibilities may include:

  • Federal legislative analysis
  • Regulatory developments
  • Committee activities
  • Executive Branch actions
  • Policy trend monitoring
  • Sector-specific issue research

This experience helps interns develop a strong understanding of the policymaking process and current federal priorities.

2. Legislative Research Memoranda

Interns will assist in preparing research memoranda that provide clients with valuable insights into legislative and policy developments.

These documents may cover:

  • Pending legislation
  • Congressional priorities
  • Committee activities
  • Political developments
  • Policy implications for clients

Developing these materials strengthens research, writing, and analytical skills.

3. Client Deliverables Support

Interns will contribute to the preparation of professional deliverables provided to clients.

This may include:

  • Policy updates
  • Legislative summaries
  • Briefing materials
  • Strategic reports
  • Issue analyses
  • Business development documents

The experience provides exposure to the consulting and government relations industries.

4. Major Legislation Tracking

Monitoring legislative activity is a key component of government affairs work.

Interns will assist with:

  • Tracking bills in Congress
  • Monitoring amendments
  • Following committee actions
  • Identifying legislative developments
  • Preparing legislative status reports

This responsibility provides firsthand exposure to the legislative process.

5. Congressional Hearing Coverage

One of the most valuable aspects of the internship is the opportunity to follow congressional proceedings.

Interns will:

  • Monitor House hearings
  • Monitor Senate hearings
  • Draft hearing summaries
  • Identify policy implications
  • Prepare client-facing briefing documents

This role enables participants to observe policymaking in action.

6. Client Meeting and Call Support

Interns will participate in client communications and strategic discussions.

Responsibilities include:

  • Attending client calls
  • Taking detailed meeting notes
  • Tracking deliverables
  • Documenting action items
  • Supporting follow-up activities

These experiences help develop professional communication and project management skills.

7. Business Development Assistance

Interns may also support business development activities through preparation of:

  • Proposal documents
  • Pitch materials
  • Strategic presentations
  • Statements of work
  • Client engagement materials

This exposure provides valuable insight into the business side of government affairs consulting.

Desired Qualifications

Elevate Government Affairs is seeking motivated individuals who demonstrate a strong interest in public policy and government.

Ideal candidates should possess:

Strong Communication Skills

Applicants should be capable of:

  • Writing clearly and professionally
  • Communicating effectively with colleagues and clients
  • Synthesizing complex information
  • Producing concise policy summaries

Excellent Attention to Detail

Government affairs work often requires precision and accuracy.

Candidates should be comfortable:

  • Reviewing legislative documents
  • Managing multiple assignments
  • Tracking policy developments
  • Producing accurate deliverables

Analytical Thinking

Successful interns should be able to:

  • Analyze policy developments
  • Identify key legislative trends
  • Evaluate policy implications
  • Conduct independent research

Organizational Skills

Interns must be able to manage competing priorities while meeting deadlines.

Knowledge of Government Structures

Candidates should have at least a foundational understanding of:

  • The U.S. Congress
  • The House of Representatives
  • The Senate
  • Congressional Committees
  • The Executive Branch
  • Federal policymaking processes

Desire to Learn

Perhaps most importantly, successful applicants should demonstrate:

  • Curiosity
  • Initiative
  • Professionalism
  • Adaptability
  • A willingness to learn new skills

While previous congressional internship experience is preferred, it is not a requirement.

Why This Internship Stands Out

This opportunity offers significantly more than administrative internship experience.

Participants gain:

  • Real-world exposure to federal policymaking
  • Direct interaction with government affairs professionals
  • Congressional monitoring experience
  • Legislative research skills
  • Client-facing experience
  • Professional writing experience
  • Policy analysis training
  • Networking opportunities
  • Exposure to multiple policy sectors

These experiences can serve as a strong foundation for careers in:

  • Government affairs
  • Public policy
  • Legislative affairs
  • Political consulting
  • Public administration
  • Advocacy
  • Lobbying
  • Public affairs
  • Strategic communications

Internship Benefits

Key benefits of the internship include:

  • Paid internship opportunity
  • Full-time professional experience
  • Direct exposure to federal policymaking
  • Congressional hearing coverage experience
  • Client engagement opportunities
  • Legislative research experience
  • Professional networking opportunities
  • Mentorship from experienced government affairs professionals
  • Career development in public policy and government relations

Application Process

Interested candidates should submit:

  1. Resume
  2. Cover Letter

Applicants must combine both documents into a single PDF file and send them via email to:

ethan@elevatega.com

Candidates should ensure that application materials clearly demonstrate their interest in federal policy, government affairs, research, communication, and legislative processes.

Important Dates

Application Deadline: Not specified

Internship Start Date: August 31, 2026

Internship End Date: December 18, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible since positions may be filled on a rolling basis.

How This Opportunity Can Accelerate Your Career

For aspiring policy professionals, few opportunities provide the combination of legislative exposure, client engagement, policy research, and professional development offered by Elevate Government Affairs.

Whether your goal is to work in Congress, public affairs, advocacy, consulting, government agencies, or policy research, this internship can provide practical experience and professional connections that help launch a successful career in government and public policy.

 

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Internship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Elevate Government Affairs Internship 2026: Paid Full-Time Government Affairs Internship in Federal Policy, Congress, Transportation, Technology, and Energy Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNESCO Fellowships 2026: Over 450 Fully Funded International Research, Training, and Innovation Opportunities for Students, PhD Candidates, Young Professionals, and Project Leaders Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
UNESCO continues to empower the next generation of researchers, innovators, professionals, and changemakers through its extensive fellowship programmes. Supported by donors and extrabudgetary funding, UNESCO Fellowships provide financial assistance for research, academic studies, professional training, and innovative projects that contribute to UNESCO’s mission of advancing education, science, culture, communication, and sustainable development worldwide. With more […]

The post UNESCO Fellowships 2026: Over 450 Fully Funded International Research, Training, and Innovation Opportunities for Students, PhD Candidates, Young Professionals, and Project Leaders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
UNESCO continues to empower the next generation of researchers, innovators, professionals, and changemakers through its extensive fellowship programmes. Supported by donors and extrabudgetary funding, UNESCO Fellowships provide financial assistance for research, academic studies, professional training, and innovative projects that contribute to UNESCO’s mission of advancing education, science, culture, communication, and sustainable development worldwide.

With more than 450 fellowships available across multiple programmes, UNESCO offers one of the most diverse international fellowship portfolios for students, PhD candidates, postdoctoral researchers, early-career professionals, and project initiators seeking to expand their knowledge, strengthen their expertise, and contribute meaningful solutions to global challenges.

These fellowships provide access to world-class institutions, international networks, advanced research opportunities, practical training experiences, and funding support that enable beneficiaries to make lasting impacts in their respective fields.

Why UNESCO Fellowships Matter

UNESCO fellowships are designed to foster knowledge creation, capacity development, international cooperation, and innovation. Through partnerships with governments, universities, research institutions, foundations, and private-sector organizations, UNESCO supports talented individuals who demonstrate the potential to contribute to sustainable development and positive societal transformation.

The fellowships focus on:

  • Advancing scientific research
  • Supporting higher education
  • Promoting international collaboration
  • Encouraging innovation and entrepreneurship
  • Strengthening professional skills
  • Supporting youth leadership
  • Preserving cultural heritage
  • Advancing sustainable development goals
  • Empowering underrepresented researchers and professionals

Successful fellows gain valuable exposure to international research environments while developing skills and networks that can accelerate their careers.

UNESCO Fellowships for Students and PhD Candidates

UNESCO offers several fellowship opportunities specifically designed for students and doctoral researchers seeking advanced academic and research experiences.

UNESCO/People’s Republic of China (The Great Wall) Fellowships

One of UNESCO’s most established fellowship programmes, the Great Wall Fellowship supports advanced studies and research opportunities in China.

Key highlights include:

  • 75 fellowships available
  • Advanced academic studies
  • Research opportunities
  • International cultural exchange
  • Access to leading Chinese institutions
  • Professional and academic development

The programme strengthens educational cooperation while supporting future leaders and researchers from around the world.

TWAS PhD Fellowships

The World Academy of Sciences (TWAS), in partnership with UNESCO, provides opportunities for doctoral candidates pursuing advanced scientific research.

Programme highlights:

  • 160 fellowships available
  • Support for PhD studies
  • Advanced scientific research opportunities
  • International collaboration
  • Capacity building in science and technology

These fellowships aim to strengthen scientific excellence and research capacity globally.

OWSD PhD Fellowships

The Organization for Women in Science for the Developing World (OWSD) supports women pursuing doctoral studies in scientific disciplines.

The programme seeks to:

  • Increase women’s participation in science
  • Promote gender equality in research
  • Support scientific innovation
  • Strengthen leadership among women scientists

UNESCO/ISEDC (Russian Federation) Co-Sponsored Fellowships

Supported by the International Centre for Sustainable Energy Development, these fellowships focus on sustainable energy and related development challenges.

Programme features:

  • 20 fellowships available
  • Specialized technical training
  • Sustainable energy studies
  • Capacity development
  • Knowledge exchange opportunities

Early Career Fellowships for Emerging Researchers and Professionals

UNESCO recognizes the importance of supporting professionals during the early stages of their careers.

UNESCO/People’s Republic of China (The Great Wall) Fellowships

The Great Wall programme also offers opportunities for early-career professionals seeking specialized training and research experiences.

UNESCO/Poland Co-Sponsored Fellowships in Engineering

These fellowships support emerging engineering professionals and researchers.

Highlights include:

  • 28 fellowships available
  • Advanced engineering training
  • Research collaboration opportunities
  • Professional development
  • Access to Polish academic institutions

UNESCO/Poland Co-Sponsored Fellowships in Archaeology and Conservation

This specialized programme supports heritage preservation professionals.

Key features:

  • 7 fellowships available
  • Archaeological research opportunities
  • Conservation training
  • Heritage management skills development
  • International collaboration

UNESCO/Republic of Korea Co-Sponsored Fellowships

The Republic of Korea partners with UNESCO to support young professionals through advanced training opportunities.

Programme highlights:

  • 25 fellowships available
  • Professional capacity building
  • Research opportunities
  • Technical training
  • International networking

UNESCO/Keizo Obuchi – Japan Young Researchers Fellowships

Named after former Japanese Prime Minister Keizo Obuchi, this fellowship supports promising young researchers pursuing innovative studies that contribute to global development.

Benefits may include:

  • Research funding
  • International recognition
  • Academic mentorship
  • Global networking opportunities

UNESCO/Kingdoms Institute – Kingdom of Saudi Arabia AlUla Fellowships Programme

This fellowship programme focuses on advancing research and innovation connected to heritage, archaeology, and cultural preservation.

Highlights include:

  • 10 fellowships available
  • Specialized research support
  • International collaboration
  • Heritage and cultural studies opportunities

TWAS Postdoctoral Fellowships

TWAS supports researchers who have completed their doctoral studies and wish to pursue advanced scientific research.

Programme highlights:

  • 100 fellowships available
  • Postdoctoral research opportunities
  • Scientific capacity development
  • International research collaboration

OWSD Early Career Fellowship

Designed specifically for women scientists, this programme supports research excellence and leadership development.

The fellowship aims to:

  • Strengthen research careers
  • Support women in STEM fields
  • Enhance scientific leadership
  • Promote gender equity in research

Silk Roads Youth Research Grant

This initiative encourages young researchers to explore topics related to the historic Silk Roads and their contemporary relevance.

Programme highlights:

  • 12 fellowships available
  • Youth-focused research support
  • Intercultural dialogue promotion
  • Historical and contemporary research opportunities

Fellowships Supporting Innovative Projects

UNESCO also supports individuals and teams working on transformative projects with the potential to address global challenges.

Green Chemistry for Life – PhosAgro/UNESCO/IUPAC

This programme promotes sustainable scientific innovation through green chemistry research.

Key objectives include:

  • Supporting environmentally friendly technologies
  • Advancing sustainable chemistry
  • Encouraging scientific innovation
  • Addressing global environmental challenges

UNESCO/Nestlé – Because Youth Matter

This fellowship supports youth-led initiatives and innovative projects designed to create positive social impact.

Programme highlights:

  • 20 fellowships available
  • Support for youth innovators
  • Funding for impactful projects
  • Leadership development opportunities
  • Social entrepreneurship support

Additional Fellowship Opportunities

GEM Report Fellowship

UNESCO also offers opportunities through the Global Education Monitoring (GEM) Report Fellowship, which supports professionals and researchers working on education-related issues and policy analysis.

Participants may gain:

  • Research experience
  • Policy engagement opportunities
  • International visibility
  • Professional networking

Benefits of UNESCO Fellowships

Recipients of UNESCO Fellowships may benefit from:

  • Financial support for research and studies
  • International training opportunities
  • Access to leading academic institutions
  • Professional mentorship
  • Global networking opportunities
  • Enhanced career prospects
  • Exposure to international best practices
  • Research collaboration opportunities
  • Leadership development
  • Increased visibility and recognition

How UNESCO Fellowships Contribute to Global Development

UNESCO fellowships play a crucial role in supporting individuals who are working toward solutions in areas such as:

  • Education
  • Science and technology
  • Sustainable development
  • Climate action
  • Cultural preservation
  • Heritage conservation
  • Innovation and entrepreneurship
  • Gender equality
  • Youth empowerment

By investing in talented individuals, UNESCO contributes to building stronger institutions, advancing knowledge, and creating sustainable solutions that benefit communities worldwide.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should regularly monitor UNESCO’s fellowship portal and the specific programme pages for application openings, eligibility criteria, deadlines, required documentation, and selection procedures.

Since each fellowship programme has its own application timeline and requirements, candidates are encouraged to review the details carefully before submitting an application.

 

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post UNESCO Fellowships 2026: Over 450 Fully Funded International Research, Training, and Innovation Opportunities for Students, PhD Candidates, Young Professionals, and Project Leaders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Horizon Europe Cluster 3 Civil Security for Society 2026: European Commission Opens Major Research Funding Opportunities with Deadline of 5 November 2026
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The European Commission, through the European Research Executive Agency (REA), has launched a major funding opportunity under Horizon Europe Cluster 3: Civil Security for Society. This initiative aims to strengthen Europe’s capacity to address growing security challenges through cutting-edge research, innovation, and collaboration among researchers, institutions, public authorities, and industry stakeholders. With proposals due by […]

The post Horizon Europe Cluster 3 Civil Security for Society 2026: European Commission Opens Major Research Funding Opportunities with Deadline of 5 November 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The European Commission, through the European Research Executive Agency (REA), has launched a major funding opportunity under Horizon Europe Cluster 3: Civil Security for Society. This initiative aims to strengthen Europe’s capacity to address growing security challenges through cutting-edge research, innovation, and collaboration among researchers, institutions, public authorities, and industry stakeholders.

With proposals due by 5 November 2026, this funding programme offers organizations the opportunity to secure support for projects that contribute to crime prevention, counter-terrorism efforts, cybersecurity, disaster resilience, border management, and critical infrastructure protection.

As global security threats continue to evolve, the Horizon Europe Cluster 3 programme seeks innovative solutions that enhance the safety, resilience, and security of societies across Europe and beyond.

About Horizon Europe Cluster 3: Civil Security for Society

Horizon Europe is the European Union’s flagship research and innovation framework programme. Within this framework, Cluster 3 focuses specifically on “Civil Security for Society.”

The programme responds to a broad range of contemporary security challenges, including:

  • Cybercrime and cybersecurity threats
  • Terrorism and organized crime
  • Border security challenges
  • Natural disasters
  • Human-made disasters
  • Protection of critical infrastructure
  • Privacy and trust in digital systems
  • Crisis management and emergency response

Through strategic investments in research and innovation, the European Commission aims to support solutions that help build safer, more resilient, and more secure societies.

Funding Areas Available in 2026

Applicants can submit proposals under several thematic areas within Cluster 3.

1. Better Protect the EU and its Citizens against Crime and Terrorism

This funding area supports projects designed to:

  • Prevent and combat crime
  • Counter terrorism and radicalization
  • Improve law enforcement capabilities
  • Develop innovative security technologies
  • Strengthen intelligence and investigative tools
  • Enhance public safety measures

Projects should contribute to a safer environment for European citizens while respecting fundamental rights and democratic values.

2. Effective Management of External Borders

This topic supports innovative solutions that improve border management and security.

Potential project areas include:

  • Smart border technologies
  • Border surveillance systems
  • Identity management solutions
  • Migration management support tools
  • Risk assessment technologies
  • Cross-border cooperation mechanisms

The goal is to improve the effectiveness and efficiency of external border operations while ensuring compliance with legal and ethical standards.

3. Resilient Infrastructures

Critical infrastructure forms the backbone of modern societies. This funding area supports projects focused on:

  • Infrastructure resilience
  • Protection of energy systems
  • Transportation security
  • Water supply protection
  • Communication networks security
  • Risk prevention and mitigation
  • Infrastructure recovery and continuity planning

Projects should strengthen society’s ability to withstand and recover from disruptions and attacks.

4. A Disaster Resilient Society for Europe

Natural disasters and emergencies continue to pose significant challenges worldwide. This funding stream supports projects aimed at:

  • Disaster risk reduction
  • Early warning systems
  • Emergency preparedness
  • Climate-related disaster resilience
  • Crisis response technologies
  • Community resilience initiatives
  • Disaster recovery strategies

Successful projects will contribute to improving Europe’s preparedness and response capacity.

5. Strengthened Security Research and Innovation (SSRI)

This thematic area focuses on strengthening Europe’s overall security research ecosystem through:

  • Research capacity building
  • Innovation acceleration
  • Knowledge transfer
  • Stakeholder collaboration
  • Policy support
  • Security innovation ecosystems
  • Technology adoption and scaling

Projects are expected to bridge the gap between research findings and practical implementation.

Deadline for Applications

Application Deadline: 5 November 2026

Applicants are encouraged to begin consortium building and proposal preparation as early as possible due to the complexity and competitiveness of Horizon Europe funding opportunities.

Who Should Consider Applying?

This opportunity is particularly relevant for:

  • Universities
  • Research institutions
  • Security agencies
  • Public authorities
  • Technology companies
  • Cybersecurity firms
  • Innovation hubs
  • SMEs
  • Civil society organizations
  • Non-governmental organizations
  • International research partnerships
  • Disaster management agencies
  • Border management authorities
  • Law enforcement organizations

Collaborative projects involving multiple organizations and countries are often encouraged under Horizon Europe.

Support Available for Applicants

The European Research Executive Agency provides extensive support to applicants throughout the proposal preparation process.

Available resources include:

Guidance on Building a Consortium

Applicants can access support and recommendations on:

  • Finding suitable project partners
  • Creating multidisciplinary teams
  • Building strong international collaborations
  • Structuring effective consortia

Application Process Guidance

Prospective applicants can obtain information regarding:

  • Proposal preparation
  • Funding rules
  • Eligible project types
  • Evaluation criteria
  • Submission procedures

Open Science Requirements

Applicants must ensure compliance with Horizon Europe principles relating to:

  • Open access publishing
  • Research data management
  • Transparency and reproducibility
  • Knowledge sharing

Gender Equality Requirements

Projects must address Horizon Europe requirements concerning:

  • Gender equality considerations
  • Inclusive research practices
  • Gender-sensitive approaches where relevant

Managing a Successful Horizon Europe Project

Organizations that receive funding will need to effectively manage project implementation.

The programme offers guidance on:

Project Management

Including:

  • Grant agreement compliance
  • Financial management
  • Consortium coordination
  • Deliverable management
  • Reporting obligations

Communication Activities

Beneficiaries are expected to:

  • Communicate project achievements
  • Increase visibility of project outcomes
  • Engage stakeholders
  • Promote public awareness

Dissemination and Exploitation

Projects should develop strategies for:

  • Sharing research findings
  • Maximizing impact
  • Supporting policy development
  • Commercializing innovations where appropriate

Policy Impact

The programme also provides guidance on how project results can effectively inform policymakers and contribute to evidence-based decision-making.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Security challenges today are increasingly interconnected and complex. Cyber threats, organized crime, infrastructure vulnerabilities, climate-related disasters, and emerging technologies require innovative responses supported by robust research and collaboration.

Through Horizon Europe Cluster 3, the European Union is investing in transformative ideas that can strengthen security, resilience, and societal well-being while fostering technological advancement and scientific excellence.

For organizations working at the intersection of security, innovation, technology, and public policy, this funding opportunity provides a valuable platform to develop impactful solutions and contribute to a safer future.

Official Link

European Research Executive Agency (REA):

Apply here

  

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Internship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Horizon Europe Cluster 3 Civil Security for Society 2026: European Commission Opens Major Research Funding Opportunities with Deadline of 5 November 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded Matsumae International Foundation Research Fellowship 2027 in Japan: Six-Month Research Opportunity for Global Scholars
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Researchers seeking international collaboration, advanced research facilities, and meaningful academic exchange now have an exceptional opportunity to conduct fully funded research in Japan through the Matsumae International Foundation (MIF) Research Fellowship Program 2027. The prestigious fellowship supports outstanding non-Japanese researchers by providing financial support, research opportunities, and access to world-class Japanese universities and research institutions. […]

The post Fully Funded Matsumae International Foundation Research Fellowship 2027 in Japan: Six-Month Research Opportunity for Global Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers seeking international collaboration, advanced research facilities, and meaningful academic exchange now have an exceptional opportunity to conduct fully funded research in Japan through the Matsumae International Foundation (MIF) Research Fellowship Program 2027.

The prestigious fellowship supports outstanding non-Japanese researchers by providing financial support, research opportunities, and access to world-class Japanese universities and research institutions. Successful fellows will spend six months in Japan conducting research while building international partnerships and contributing to scientific advancement.

The fellowship is designed to promote mutual understanding, academic cooperation, and long-term relationships between Japan and researchers from around the world.

Applications for the 2027 fellowship cycle are currently open.

About the Matsumae International Foundation Research Fellowship

The Matsumae International Foundation (MIF) was established in 1979 by Dr. Shigeyoshi Matsumae, the founder of Tokai University. The foundation was created with a vision of promoting global peace and international friendship through academic exchange and research collaboration.

For more than four decades, the fellowship has welcomed talented researchers from around the world to Japan, providing opportunities to undertake advanced research projects while fostering cultural understanding and international cooperation.

The program enables researchers to work closely with Japanese academics and institutions, helping participants expand their expertise and strengthen their professional networks.

Fellowship Duration

Selected fellows will conduct research in Japan for:

  • Six months

Research activities may be undertaken during the fellowship period scheduled between June 2027 and March 2028.

Fellowship Benefits

The Matsumae International Foundation Research Fellowship is fully funded and offers a comprehensive support package.

Benefits include:

Monthly Allowance

Fellows receive:

  • JPY 220,000 per month

This allowance is intended to cover living expenses and support research activities during the fellowship period.

Arrival Grant

Successful applicants receive:

  • JPY 120,000 arrival allowance

This funding assists fellows with initial settlement costs upon arrival in Japan.

Round-Trip Airfare

The fellowship covers:

  • Economy-class airfare between the fellow’s home country and Japan

Insurance Coverage

Participants receive:

  • Overseas travel and medical insurance during their fellowship stay

Study Tour

Fellows may also participate in:

  • Organized cultural and academic study tours in Japan

These activities provide opportunities to learn more about Japanese society, culture, and research environments.

Research Fields

The fellowship welcomes research proposals from a broad range of academic disciplines.

Priority is often given to research in:

  • Natural Sciences
  • Engineering
  • Medicine
  • Agriculture

The program particularly values research that can contribute to solving important challenges facing society and support development in the fellow’s home country.

Why Apply for This Fellowship?

Japan is home to some of the world’s leading universities, laboratories, and research centers. Through this fellowship, researchers gain access to:

Advanced Research Facilities

Participants can work with cutting-edge technologies and infrastructure available at Japanese institutions.

International Collaboration

The fellowship creates opportunities to collaborate with leading Japanese researchers and establish long-term academic partnerships.

Global Professional Network

Fellows become part of an international network of researchers and alumni from around the world.

Cultural Exchange

Living and working in Japan provides a unique opportunity to experience Japanese culture while strengthening international understanding.

Career Advancement

The international experience, research outputs, and professional connections developed during the fellowship can significantly strengthen a researcher’s academic career.

Who Can Apply?

Applicants must satisfy all eligibility requirements before submitting an application.

Nationality Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Be non-Japanese citizens

Academic Qualification

Applicants must:

  • Hold a PhD degree at the time of application

Host Institution Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Obtain acceptance from a host institution in Japan before applying

The host institution may include:

  • Universities
  • National research institutes
  • Public research organizations
  • Equivalent academic institutions

Language Requirement

Applicants must demonstrate sufficient ability to conduct research in:

  • English or
  • Japanese

Age Requirement

Applicants should generally be:

  • 45 years old or younger

Employment Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Be employed in their home country
  • Intend to return to their home country after completing the fellowship

Application Process

The fellowship requires applicants to secure a host institution before submitting an application.

Step 1: Identify a Host Institution

Applicants should contact professors or research groups in Japan whose work aligns with their research interests.

Step 2: Obtain Host Acceptance

Researchers must receive confirmation from a Japanese institution agreeing to host their proposed research project.

Step 3: Prepare Application Materials

Applicants should gather all required documentation, including research proposals and supporting materials.

Step 4: Submit Application

Applications must be submitted according to the instructions provided by the Matsumae International Foundation.

Application Deadline

Click here for more and to apply

Deadline: 30 June 2026

Applicants are encouraged to begin contacting potential host institutions as early as possible because obtaining host acceptance can take considerable time.

Selection Criteria

Applications are evaluated based on:

  1. Academic excellence
  2. Research achievements
  3. Quality and relevance of the research proposal
  4. Potential impact of the proposed research
  5. Feasibility of the project
  6. Contribution to international cooperation and academic exchange

Important Tip for Applicants

One of the most important components of a successful application is securing a strong host institution in Japan.

Researchers should carefully identify professors whose expertise closely matches their proposed research and establish communication early. A well-aligned host institution can significantly strengthen the overall application.

Why This Fellowship Stands Out

Unlike many research funding opportunities, the Matsumae International Foundation Research Fellowship combines generous financial support with academic independence, international collaboration, and cultural exchange.

For researchers seeking to advance their careers, access world-class facilities, and contribute to global scientific cooperation, this fellowship represents an outstanding opportunity to conduct meaningful research in one of the world’s leading research environments.

Interested applicants should begin preparing their applications and securing host institution support well before the deadline of 30 June 2026.

Click here for more global fellowship opportunities

The post Fully Funded Matsumae International Foundation Research Fellowship 2027 in Japan: Six-Month Research Opportunity for Global Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Bracken Prize for Young Authors 2026: Win £15,000 for Your Unpublished Business Book Proposal
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
If you are an ambitious young writer with a groundbreaking idea that could shape the future of business, the Bracken Prize for Young Authors 2026 presents an outstanding opportunity to gain international recognition, financial support, and industry exposure. Organized by the Financial Times, the Bracken Prize (formerly known as the Bracken Bower Prize) celebrates the […]

The post Bracken Prize for Young Authors 2026: Win £15,000 for Your Unpublished Business Book Proposal appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are an ambitious young writer with a groundbreaking idea that could shape the future of business, the Bracken Prize for Young Authors 2026 presents an outstanding opportunity to gain international recognition, financial support, and industry exposure.

Organized by the Financial Times, the Bracken Prize (formerly known as the Bracken Bower Prize) celebrates the next generation of business thinkers by recognizing exceptional unpublished business book proposals from emerging writers under the age of 35.

The competition is designed to encourage fresh thinking and bold ideas that address today’s rapidly changing business landscape. Whether your proposal explores artificial intelligence, entrepreneurship, sustainability, finance, leadership, innovation, technology, the future of work, or global economic trends, this prestigious award provides a platform to showcase your ideas to some of the world’s leading business experts.

The winner will receive £15,000, while two finalists will each receive £2,000, making it one of the most valuable international awards for aspiring business authors.

About the Bracken Prize for Young Authors

The Bracken Prize was established to encourage young writers and researchers to explore the ideas that will influence the future of business.

Previously known as the Bracken Bower Prize, the award has evolved into one of the Financial Times’ flagship initiatives supporting emerging voices in business writing.

Each year, the competition recognizes the most compelling proposal for an original business book written by an author under the age of 35.

Rather than rewarding completed manuscripts, the prize focuses on identifying outstanding ideas during the proposal stage, giving talented writers the opportunity to develop their concepts before publication.

Why the Bracken Prize Exists

Many of today’s most significant economic, technological, political, and social changes are being driven by younger generations. However, opportunities for emerging writers to develop ambitious long-form business ideas remain limited.

The Bracken Prize was created to bridge this gap by providing:

  • Recognition for emerging business thinkers.
  • Financial support for promising authors.
  • Visibility within the international publishing and business community.
  • Expert feedback on innovative business ideas.
  • A platform for challenging conventional thinking.
  • Encouragement for rigorous and evidence-based business writing.

The award particularly values proposals that challenge established ideas, explore emerging trends, and rethink the role of business in society.

Prize Awards

The Bracken Prize offers generous financial rewards:

Winner

  • Cash prize of £15,000

Finalists

  • Two finalists will each receive £2,000

Beyond the prize money, finalists gain valuable recognition from one of the world’s leading business media organizations, significantly increasing the visibility of their work.

What the Judges Are Looking For

Judges evaluate submissions based on several important qualities, including:

  • Originality.
  • Intellectual rigor.
  • Creativity.
  • Strong research foundation.
  • Fresh perspectives.
  • Compelling storytelling.
  • Relevance to modern business challenges.
  • Insight into future business trends.

Successful proposals should offer innovative ideas that contribute meaningfully to discussions surrounding business, economics, leadership, technology, entrepreneurship, sustainability, or related fields.

Benefits of the Bracken Prize

Participating authors enjoy numerous benefits beyond financial rewards.

These include:

  • Opportunity to win £15,000.
  • International recognition.
  • Increased credibility as an emerging business author.
  • Exposure to publishing professionals.
  • Valuable expert review of your proposal.
  • Enhanced visibility within the Financial Times network.
  • Opportunity to refine your business book concept.
  • Recognition among leading business thinkers.
  • Greater opportunities for future publishing success.

Who Should Apply?

The Bracken Prize is ideal for:

  • Young business writers.
  • Researchers.
  • Journalists.
  • Entrepreneurs.
  • Academics.
  • Consultants.
  • Economists.
  • Startup founders.
  • Innovation experts.
  • Policy researchers.
  • Professionals with original ideas about the future of business.

Applicants do not need to have previously published a book. What matters most is the originality and quality of the proposal.

Proposal Requirements

Applicants are expected to submit an original business book proposal that:

  • Has not been published.
  • Demonstrates innovative thinking.
  • Addresses important business challenges or future trends.
  • Is supported by rigorous research.
  • Offers fresh perspectives.
  • Engages contemporary business debates.

Why You Should Apply

Winning or becoming a finalist can significantly enhance an author’s career.

Benefits include:

  1. Increased professional credibility.
  2. Greater visibility within the publishing industry.
  3. Financial support to continue developing the book.
  4. Recognition from the Financial Times.
  5. Opportunities to connect with business leaders and publishing professionals.
  6. Stronger prospects for securing a publishing contract.
  7. Validation of your ideas by respected experts.

For aspiring business authors, the Bracken Prize represents an exceptional opportunity to transform an innovative idea into a published work with global impact.

Application Deadline

The deadline for submitting proposals is:

30 September 2026

Applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their proposals well in advance to ensure they submit a polished and compelling entry before the closing date.

How to Apply

Eligible applicants should prepare an original, unpublished business book proposal and submit it through the official Bracken Prize website before the deadline.

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the official eligibility requirements, submission guidelines, and Terms & Conditions before completing their application.

Apply Here

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global award opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Bracken Prize for Young Authors 2026: Win £15,000 for Your Unpublished Business Book Proposal appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GEF Small Grants Programme CSO Challenge Program 2026: Receive Up to $300,000 to Scale High-Impact Environmental Solutions
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Civil society organizations around the world are playing a critical role in addressing climate change, biodiversity loss, land degradation, pollution, and other environmental challenges. Yet many of these organizations struggle to secure the resources needed to expand successful initiatives and increase their impact. To address this gap, the GEF Small Grants Programme (SGP) CSO Challenge […]

The post GEF Small Grants Programme CSO Challenge Program 2026: Receive Up to $300,000 to Scale High-Impact Environmental Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Civil society organizations around the world are playing a critical role in addressing climate change, biodiversity loss, land degradation, pollution, and other environmental challenges. Yet many of these organizations struggle to secure the resources needed to expand successful initiatives and increase their impact.

To address this gap, the GEF Small Grants Programme (SGP) CSO Challenge Program has launched a new funding opportunity designed to identify, reward, and support innovative environmental solutions led by civil society organizations.

Supported by the Global Environment Facility (GEF) and led by the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN), the program provides grants of up to USD $300,000 to help qualified organizations scale proven environmental solutions, replicate successful models, and expand their impact.

In addition to funding, selected organizations will benefit from technical assistance, capacity-building opportunities, peer learning, strategic partnerships, and international visibility.

For the current call, applications are open for organizations operating in the Eastern Europe, Caucasus, and Central Asia (EECCA) region.

About the GEF SGP CSO Challenge Program

The GEF Small Grants Programme has a long history of supporting community-led environmental action across the globe. Building on the success of previous initiatives, the CSO Challenge Program was created to provide larger grants and more comprehensive support to organizations that have already demonstrated success and are ready to grow.

The program recognizes that civil society organizations and local communities are often at the forefront of environmental conservation and sustainable development. Their innovations, local knowledge, and grassroots approaches are essential for addressing some of the world’s most pressing environmental challenges.

The CSO Challenge Program aims to amplify these efforts by helping organizations scale their solutions, reach new communities, and create lasting environmental impact.

Funding Amount

Selected organizations can receive:

  • Grants of up to USD $300,000
  • Funding for projects lasting up to 24 months
  • Tailored technical support
  • Capacity-building assistance
  • Networking opportunities
  • Peer-to-peer learning opportunities
  • Partnership development support
  • Increased international recognition and visibility

Unlike traditional GEF Small Grants Programme funding, this initiative prioritizes larger grants that can significantly accelerate growth, replication, and scaling of successful environmental solutions.

Program Objectives

The CSO Challenge Program seeks to achieve several important goals:

1. Recognize Innovative Environmental Solutions

The program identifies and rewards organizations that have developed effective and innovative approaches to environmental conservation and sustainability.

2. Strengthen Organizational Capacity

Selected organizations receive support to enhance their ability to design, manage, and expand impactful environmental programs.

3. Scale Successful Models

The program helps organizations replicate proven approaches in new regions, communities, and contexts.

4. Encourage Knowledge Sharing

Participants are encouraged to exchange experiences, lessons learned, and best practices with other organizations and stakeholders.

5. Promote Inclusion

The program supports initiatives that actively engage:

  • Women
  • Youth
  • Indigenous Peoples
  • Local communities
  • Marginalized populations

What Types of Projects Are Funded?

Funding is available for initiatives that focus on scaling and expanding successful environmental solutions.

Supported activities include:

Replicating Proven Models

Organizations can replicate successful environmental approaches in new communities, regions, or countries.

Adapting Existing Solutions

Projects may adapt effective models to suit different local conditions and environmental contexts.

Testing Scaling Strategies

Organizations can explore innovative approaches to expanding the reach and impact of promising environmental initiatives.

Strategic Priority Areas

Applicants must work within one of the program’s five strategic priority areas.

1. Community-Based Management of Threatened Ecosystems and Species

Projects that support local communities in protecting biodiversity, restoring ecosystems, and conserving threatened species.

2. Sustainable Agriculture, Fisheries, and Food Security

Initiatives that promote sustainable food systems while protecting natural resources and strengthening livelihoods.

3. Low-Carbon Energy Access

Projects that improve access to renewable and low-carbon energy solutions while delivering environmental and social benefits.

4. Chemicals and Waste Management

Programs that reduce pollution, improve waste management systems, and protect environmental and human health.

5. Sustainable Urban Solutions

Innovative approaches that improve environmental sustainability, climate resilience, and resource efficiency in urban areas.

Who Can Apply?

Eligible applicants must meet all of the following requirements:

Civil Society Organization Status

Applicants must:

  • Be a legally registered Civil Society Organization (CSO)
  • Possess legal status in their country
  • Maintain a bank account in the organization’s name

Geographic Eligibility

The applicant organization and the initiative to be replicated or scaled must be located in an eligible GEF Recipient Country.

Eligible regions include:

Africa

  • 54 eligible countries

Latin America and the Caribbean

  • 33 eligible countries

Eastern Europe, Caucasus and Central Asia (EECCA)

  • 21 eligible countries

Asia-Pacific

  • 41 eligible countries

Why This Grant Matters

Many environmental organizations develop highly effective local solutions but lack the resources needed to expand their reach. The CSO Challenge Program addresses this challenge by investing in organizations that have already demonstrated success and are ready for growth.

Through funding, mentoring, partnerships, and technical support, the program helps organizations move from local success stories to scalable models capable of generating regional and global impact.

Application Process

The CSO Challenge Program uses a two-stage selection process.

Stage 1: Concept Note Submission

Applicants must submit a Concept Note outlining their proposed project and scaling strategy.

Stage 2: Full Proposal Submission

Organizations shortlisted during the first stage will be invited to prepare and submit a comprehensive Full Proposal.

A diverse selection committee comprising representatives from:

  • The GEF Secretariat
  • Civil society organizations
  • Indigenous Peoples’ organizations
  • Youth organizations
  • Women’s organizations
  • IUCN

will evaluate submissions and select finalists and winners.

Languages Accepted

Applications may be submitted in:

  • English
  • French
  • Spanish

Application Timeline

Step 1: Concept Note Stage

05 June 2026

  • Call for proposals opens

20 June 2026

  • Deadline for questions

20 July 2026

  • Deadline to submit Concept Notes

Step 2: Full Proposal Stage

19 August 2026

  • Finalists notified and invited to submit Full Proposals

18 September 2026

  • Deadline for Full Proposal submission

Step 3: Final Selection

18 October 2026

  • Winners and finalists notified

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on:

  1. Alignment with SGP 2.0 priority areas
  2. Impact potential
  3. Alignment with national Multilateral Environmental Agreement (MEA) commitments
  4. Scalability
  5. Inclusivity
  6. Organizational capacity and sustainability

Benefits Beyond Funding

In addition to financial support, successful applicants will gain access to:

  • Expert technical assistance
  • Organizational capacity strengthening
  • International exposure
  • Peer learning opportunities
  • Strategic partnerships
  • Networking with governments, donors, and private sector actors
  • Knowledge-sharing platforms

Organizations Behind the Program

Global Environment Facility (GEF)

The GEF is one of the world’s leading environmental funding institutions. Over more than three decades, it has provided over $26 billion in financing and mobilized an additional $148 billion to support environmental projects globally.

International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN)

IUCN is the world’s largest and oldest global environmental network, working with governments, civil society organizations, and experts in over 160 countries to address environmental and development challenges.

RTI International

RTI International is a globally recognized independent scientific research institute that supports evidence-based solutions across environmental, social, and technical disciplines.

Application Deadline

Concept Note Deadline:

20 July 2026

Click here for more and to apply

Interested organizations are encouraged to review the Call for Proposals, Concept Note Template, and Guidelines for Applicants before beginning their application process.

Organizations with proven environmental impact and ambitions to scale their solutions now have a unique opportunity to secure up to $300,000 in funding and join a global network of environmental change-makers working to build a more sustainable future.

Click here for more global grant opportunities

The post GEF Small Grants Programme CSO Challenge Program 2026: Receive Up to $300,000 to Scale High-Impact Environmental Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 2026 EU Gender Equality Champions Award: Win Up to €100,000 and Gain European Recognition for Advancing Equality in Research and Innovation
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The European Commission has officially opened applications for the prestigious 2026 EU Gender Equality Champions Award, a major initiative designed to recognize and celebrate institutions that are making significant progress in advancing gender equality across research, innovation, leadership, and higher education. With a total prize fund of €400,000 available, the award highlights organizations that are […]

The post 2026 EU Gender Equality Champions Award: Win Up to €100,000 and Gain European Recognition for Advancing Equality in Research and Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The European Commission has officially opened applications for the prestigious 2026 EU Gender Equality Champions Award, a major initiative designed to recognize and celebrate institutions that are making significant progress in advancing gender equality across research, innovation, leadership, and higher education.

With a total prize fund of €400,000 available, the award highlights organizations that are embedding gender equality into their institutional culture, governance structures, research environments, and innovation ecosystems. The initiative forms part of the European Union’s broader commitment to creating a more inclusive, diverse, and competitive European Research Area.

A significant development for the 2026 edition is the expansion of eligibility criteria. For the first time, research and innovation organizations from EU candidate countries can participate alongside institutions from EU Member States and Horizon Europe Associated Countries.

Organizations interested in applying must submit their applications by 29 September 2026 through the EU Funding and Tenders Portal.

About the 2026 EU Gender Equality Champions Award

Established in 2022 by the European Commission’s Directorate-General for Research and Innovation, the EU Gender Equality Champions Award recognizes institutions that have successfully implemented transformative gender equality measures within their organizations.

The award aims to encourage structural and sustainable change by highlighting successful institutional practices that promote equal opportunities, diversity, inclusion, and fairness within research and innovation environments.

By rewarding excellence and innovation in gender equality, the European Commission seeks to inspire organizations across Europe and beyond to adopt ambitious Gender Equality Plans (GEPs) and implement meaningful reforms that improve workplace culture, leadership representation, and research excellence.

Why Gender Equality Matters in Research and Innovation

Gender equality is increasingly recognized as a critical factor in strengthening research quality, innovation outcomes, and institutional performance.

Research environments that embrace diversity benefit from:

  • Broader perspectives and ideas
  • Improved decision-making processes
  • Enhanced research quality
  • Greater innovation potential
  • Increased institutional competitiveness
  • More inclusive leadership structures
  • Better opportunities for underrepresented groups

The European Commission considers gender equality not only a social objective but also a strategic advantage that contributes to Europe’s long-term competitiveness and scientific excellence.

Prize Value and Benefits

Successful applicants will receive substantial recognition and financial support through the award program.

Benefits include:

1. Financial Awards

Winning institutions can receive prizes worth up to €100,000.

The total prize fund for the 2026 edition amounts to €400,000.

2. International Recognition

Award recipients gain visibility across Europe’s research and innovation community, positioning themselves as leaders in gender equality and institutional transformation.

3. Enhanced Institutional Reputation

Recognition from the European Commission can strengthen an institution’s profile among:

  • Researchers
  • Students
  • Policymakers
  • Funding agencies
  • International partners
  • Industry collaborators

4. Showcase of Best Practices

Winning organizations serve as examples of successful gender equality implementation, helping inspire similar initiatives across Europe and beyond.

5. Networking Opportunities

Award recipients gain opportunities to connect with influential stakeholders in European research, innovation, and higher education.

Award Categories

Applicants may apply to only one category.

1. Sustainable Gender Equality Champions

This category recognizes organizations that have demonstrated a strong and long-term commitment to gender equality through sustained implementation of Gender Equality Plans.

Eligible organizations should show:

  • Significant structural achievements
  • Long-term institutional impact
  • Demonstrated progress over multiple years
  • Sustainable gender equality outcomes

Number of Winners:

  • One winner

Prize:

  • €100,000

2. Newcomer Gender Equality Champions

This category celebrates institutions that have recently completed their first Gender Equality Plan and achieved significant progress in implementation.

Organizations should demonstrate:

  • Successful launch of their first GEP
  • Strong early-stage results
  • Effective implementation strategies
  • Clear institutional commitment

Number of Winners:

  • Two winners

Prize:

  • €100,000 per winner

3. Inclusive Gender Equality Champions

This category recognizes organizations that go beyond gender equality by addressing multiple dimensions of inclusion and diversity.

Applicants should demonstrate efforts that combine gender equality with at least two additional social categories, including:

  • Racial or ethnic origin
  • Disability
  • Sexual orientation
  • Social origin
  • Other dimensions of diversity and inclusion

Number of Winners:

  • One winner

Prize:

  • €100,000

Important Eligibility Rules

Organizations planning to apply should carefully review all eligibility requirements before submitting an application.

Key rules include:

  • Applicants may apply to only one category.
  • Previous award winners are not eligible to participate again.
  • Organizations must operate within research and innovation sectors.
  • Institutions must satisfy category-specific criteria.
  • Applications must comply with the official Rules of Contest.

What Is a Gender Equality Plan (GEP)?

A Gender Equality Plan (GEP) is a strategic framework that outlines measures and objectives aimed at improving gender equality within an organization.

Typical components include:

  • Gender-balanced leadership and governance
  • Equal career progression opportunities
  • Inclusive recruitment processes
  • Work-life balance measures
  • Prevention of gender-based discrimination
  • Gender-sensitive research and teaching practices
  • Monitoring and evaluation systems

Under Horizon Europe, many public bodies, research organizations, and higher education institutions are required to maintain active Gender Equality Plans to qualify for certain research funding opportunities.

Application Process

Organizations must submit their applications electronically through the EU Funding and Tenders Portal.

Application Steps

  1. Review the official Rules of Contest.
  2. Identify the most suitable award category.
  3. Gather evidence of gender equality achievements.
  4. Prepare all required supporting documentation.
  5. Complete the online application form.
  6. Submit the application before the deadline.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 29 September 2026

Late submissions will not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to begin preparations well in advance.

Selection Process

After the submission deadline, applications will be assessed by an independent jury composed of international experts.

The evaluation will focus on:

  • Impact
  • Innovation
  • Sustainability
  • Institutional commitment
  • Quality of implementation
  • Inclusiveness

Successful organizations will be selected based on their demonstrated achievements and long-term contributions to gender equality.

Announcement of Winners

The winners of the 2026 EU Gender Equality Champions Award will be officially announced during a dedicated award ceremony in Brussels in 2027.

The ceremony will celebrate institutions that are driving meaningful cultural and structural change across Europe’s research and innovation landscape.

Why Organizations Should Apply

The 2026 EU Gender Equality Champions Award represents more than a financial prize. It provides an opportunity for institutions to gain European recognition for their commitment to equality, diversity, and inclusion.

Organizations that have invested in building equitable workplaces, inclusive leadership structures, and transformative Gender Equality Plans can use this platform to showcase their achievements, inspire others, and contribute to a stronger and more inclusive European research ecosystem.

With expanded eligibility now including EU candidate countries, the 2026 edition offers a unique opportunity for a wider range of institutions to join Europe’s growing movement toward gender equality and institutional excellence.

How to Apply

Click here for more and to apply

Interested organizations should submit their applications through the EU Funding and Tenders Portal before 29 September 2026.

Click here for more global Award opportunities

 

The post 2026 EU Gender Equality Champions Award: Win Up to €100,000 and Gain European Recognition for Advancing Equality in Research and Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University 2027: Fully Funded Master’s Scholarships for African Leaders in Public Health, Public Policy, Nutrition, and Sustainable Agriculture
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications Opening Soon for One of Canada’s Most Prestigious Fully Funded Graduate Scholarship Programs for African Students The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University is now preparing to launch its 2027 application cycle, offering a transformative opportunity for talented young African leaders to pursue fully funded master’s studies at one of Canada’s most prestigious […]

The post Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University 2027: Fully Funded Master’s Scholarships for African Leaders in Public Health, Public Policy, Nutrition, and Sustainable Agriculture appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Opening Soon for One of Canada’s Most Prestigious Fully Funded Graduate Scholarship Programs for African Students

The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University is now preparing to launch its 2027 application cycle, offering a transformative opportunity for talented young African leaders to pursue fully funded master’s studies at one of Canada’s most prestigious universities.

Hosted by McGill University in Montreal, Quebec, Canada, this highly competitive scholarship program is designed to support outstanding African students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement, leadership potential, commitment to community service, and a strong desire to contribute to the social and economic development of Africa.

The scholarship is far more than a financial award. It is a comprehensive leadership and educational initiative that empowers scholars to become agents of change capable of transforming sectors such as public health, nutrition, public policy, food systems, environmental sustainability, and agriculture across the African continent.

Each year, the program awards approximately 35 fully funded scholarships across 13 eligible graduate programs, covering all major educational and living expenses while providing leadership development opportunities and access to one of the world’s leading academic communities.

About the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University

The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program is a global initiative that seeks to develop the next generation of ethical and transformative leaders.

At McGill University, the program specifically focuses on preparing African scholars to address critical development challenges in:

  • Sustainable agriculture
  • Food security
  • Public health
  • Nutrition
  • Environmental management
  • Water resources
  • Biotechnology
  • Public policy

The program recognizes that Africa’s future depends heavily on investing in talented young people who can drive innovation, influence policy, strengthen institutions, and improve the well-being of communities across the continent.

By combining academic excellence with leadership training, community engagement, and global networking opportunities, the scholarship creates pathways for scholars to make lasting contributions both during and after their studies.

What Makes the McGill Mastercard Foundation Scholarship Unique?

Unlike many traditional scholarships that focus only on tuition support, the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program offers a holistic support system designed to remove financial barriers and help scholars succeed academically, professionally, and personally.

The scholarship places strong emphasis on:

  • Leadership development
  • Community impact
  • Social responsibility
  • Academic excellence
  • Sustainable development
  • Long-term contribution to Africa

Successful scholars become part of a global network of Mastercard Foundation Scholars and alumni working to create positive change in their communities and countries.

Scholarship Benefits

The Mastercard Foundation Scholarship at McGill University is fully comprehensive and covers virtually all expenses associated with graduate studies in Canada.

Benefits include:

Full Tuition Coverage

The scholarship covers the complete international student tuition fees required to pursue an eligible master’s degree at McGill University.

On-Campus Housing

Scholars receive accommodation support through on-campus housing arrangements, helping ensure a smooth transition into university life.

Monthly Living Stipend

Recipients receive financial support to cover living expenses throughout their studies.

Academic Resources and Learning Support

The program provides access to academic tools, learning materials, and support services designed to maximize academic success.

Pre-Arrival and Immigration Support

Scholars receive guidance and support throughout:

  • Immigration procedures
  • Study permit preparation
  • Travel planning
  • Arrival and settlement processes

Leadership Development Opportunities

Participants gain access to:

  • Leadership training
  • Mentorship opportunities
  • Professional development activities
  • Networking events
  • Community engagement initiatives

Access to a Global Scholars Network

Students become members of an international community of Mastercard Foundation Scholars, opening doors to lifelong collaborations and opportunities.

Number of Scholarships Available

McGill University offers:

35 Scholarships Annually

These scholarships are distributed across 13 eligible graduate programs.

Competition is expected to be intense due to the program’s prestige and comprehensive funding package.

Eligible Academic Programs

The scholarship supports selected master’s programs that align with Africa’s development priorities.

Non-Thesis Programs

Eligible non-thesis programs include:

Sustainable Agriculture

  • Animal Science: Sustainable Agriculture

Environmental and Water Resources Engineering

  • Bioresource Engineering – Environmental Engineering
  • Bioresource Engineering – Integrated Water Resource Management

Food Systems and Biotechnology

  • Biotechnology
  • Food Science
  • Food Safety
  • Integrated Food & Bioprocessing

Health and Public Health

  • Human Nutrition (Project)
  • Biostatistics
  • Public Health

Public Policy

  • Public Policy

Thesis-Based Programs

Eligible thesis programs include:

  • Animal Science
  • Bioresource Engineering

Important Note for Thesis Applicants

Applicants interested in thesis-based programs must not contact professors or supervisors directly.

Instead:

  1. Apply through the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program.
  2. Wait for scholarship screening results.
  3. If selected for a fee waiver, McGill will provide instructions regarding supervisor matching.

Who Can Apply?

The scholarship targets exceptional African students who demonstrate both academic excellence and a commitment to creating positive social impact.

Applicants must:

Academic Requirements

  • Hold a bachelor’s degree or recognized equivalent.
  • Meet McGill University’s graduate admission requirements.
  • Have a degree closely related to the chosen graduate program.
  • Demonstrate strong academic performance.

Applicants with First Class Honors or equivalent qualifications are considered especially competitive.

Citizenship Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be citizens of an African country.
  • Currently reside in Africa.

Age Requirement

Applicants must be:

  • 35 years old or younger at the time of application.

Leadership and Service

Candidates should demonstrate:

  • Community involvement
  • Leadership experience
  • Volunteerism
  • Civic engagement
  • Service initiatives

Financial Need

Applicants must clearly demonstrate that financial barriers have limited or prevented access to educational opportunities.

Commitment to Africa

Strong candidates will show:

  • A desire to contribute to their communities.
  • Commitment to Africa’s development.
  • Plans to create positive impact after graduation.

Priority Groups

The program strongly encourages applications from:

Women

Women applicants are given priority consideration.

Persons with Disabilities

Applicants living with disabilities are encouraged to apply.

Displaced Populations

Priority is given to:

  • Refugees
  • Asylum seekers
  • Internally displaced persons (IDPs)
  • Returnees

Ineligible Applicants

The following individuals are not eligible:

  • Applicants who have already completed a master’s degree.
  • Applicants currently enrolled in a master’s degree.
  • Permanent residents of Canada.
  • Permanent residents of the United States.
  • Permanent residents of the United Kingdom.
  • Permanent residents of EU countries.
  • Dual citizens with these residency privileges.

Eligible Countries

The scholarship is open to citizens and residents of every country in Africa, including:

  • Ghana
  • Nigeria
  • Kenya
  • Uganda
  • Rwanda
  • Ethiopia
  • South Africa
  • Tanzania
  • Zambia
  • Zimbabwe
  • Senegal
  • Morocco
  • Algeria
  • Egypt

And all other African Union member states.

Learn more about the Eligibility HERE

Mandatory Information Sessions

One of the most important requirements of the application process is attendance at an official information session.

Is Attendance Mandatory?

Yes.

Only eligible applicants who attend an information session will receive access to the application form.

Failure to attend an information session means you cannot apply.

Information Session Schedule

North Africa & West Africa

  • June 23, 2026
  • June 25, 2026

East Africa & Southern Africa

  • June 30, 2026
  • July 2, 2026

Central Africa

  • July 7, 2026
  • July 9, 2026

Open Session for All Regions

  • July 15, 2026

Applicants should register for the session corresponding to their region.

Technology Barriers

Applicants unable to attend because of internet or technology challenges may submit a technological barrier disclosure form.

Learn more about the Info Sessions HERE

Selection Criteria

Applications are evaluated based on:

Academic Merit

Applicants must be academically competitive for admission to McGill University.

Leadership Potential

The committee seeks transformative leaders capable of driving meaningful change.

Community Service

Applicants should demonstrate active engagement in community development initiatives.

Development Impact

Strong consideration is given to candidates whose studies can positively impact their home countries.

Overcoming Barriers

Applicants who have overcome:

  • Financial barriers
  • Social barriers
  • Personal challenges
  • Health-related challenges

may receive additional consideration.

Learn more about the Selection Criteria HERE

Detailed Application Timeline

Information Sessions

June 23 – July 15, 2026

Scholarship Applications Open

August 3, 2026
(9:00 AM EST)

Scholarship Application Deadline

August 21, 2026
(4:00 PM EST)

No late applications will be accepted.

McGill Admission Stage

September – November 2026

Selected candidates receive instructions to apply to McGill University.

University Application Deadline

December 1, 2026

Only applicants who receive fee waivers can proceed.

Admission Decisions

February – March 2027

Scholarship Selection

Mid-February – Early March 2027

Scholarship Results

Late March – Early April 2027

Understanding the Selection Process

The scholarship process involves several stages.

Stage 1: Information Session

Applicants attend a mandatory session.

Stage 2: Scholarship Application

Applicants complete the Mastercard Foundation application.

Stage 3: Pre-Screening

Applications undergo eligibility assessment.

Stage 4: Fee Waiver Invitation

Successful applicants receive fee waiver instructions.

Stage 5: McGill Admission Application

Candidates submit university applications.

Stage 6: Admission Decision

McGill evaluates academic eligibility.

Stage 7: Scholarship Selection

Final scholarship recipients are selected.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

What Is the Purpose of This Scholarship?

The scholarship aims to support talented African leaders pursuing master’s studies in fields critical to Africa’s development, including public health, nutrition, public policy, and sustainable agriculture.

What Does the Scholarship Cover?

The scholarship covers:

  • Full tuition
  • Housing
  • Monthly stipend
  • Immigration support
  • Academic resources
  • Leadership development opportunities
